heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by John Ford. Written by Dudley Nichols and Ben Hecht, based on a short story by Ernest Haycox.
clean: Directed by John Ford. Written by Dudley Nichols and Ben Hecht, based on a short story by Ernest Haycox.

heading: The Ringo Kid
raw: Well, there are some things a man just can't run away from.
clean: Well, there are some things a man just can't run away from.

heading: The Ringo Kid
raw: Well, I guess you can't break out of prison and into society in the same week.
clean: Well, I guess you can't break out of prison and into society in the same week.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marshal Curly Wilcox: Come busting in here - you'd think we were being attacked! You can find another wife.
clean: Come busting in here - you'd think we were being attacked! You can find another wife.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Chris: Sure I can find another wife. But she take my rifle and my horse. Oh, I'll never sell her. I love her so much. I beat her with a whip and she never get tired.
clean: Sure I can find another wife. But she take my rifle and my horse. Oh, I'll never sell her. I love her so much. I beat her with a whip and she never get tired.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dr. Josiah Boone: Your wife?
clean: Your wife?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Chris: No, my horse. I can find another wife easy, yes, but not a horse like that!
clean: No, my horse. I can find another wife easy, yes, but not a horse like that!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dr. Josiah Boone: Jerry, I'll admit as one man to another that, economically, I haven't been of much value to you. But do you suppose you could put one on credit?
clean: Jerry, I'll admit as one man to another that, economically, I haven't been of much value to you. But do you suppose you could put one on credit?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jerry (bartender): If talk was money, Doc, you'd be the best customer I got.
clean: Jerry (bartender): If talk was money, Doc, you'd be the best customer I got.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Buck: If I was you, I'd let them shoot it out.
clean: If I was you, I'd let them shoot it out.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marshal Curly Wilcox: Let who?
clean: Let who?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Buck: Luke Plummer and the Kid. There would be a lot more peace in this territory if that Luke Plummer had so many holes in him he couldn't hold his liquor.
clean: Luke Plummer and the Kid. There would be a lot more peace in this territory if that Luke Plummer had so many holes in him he couldn't hold his liquor.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ed (editor): McCoy! Billy, kill that story about the Republican Convention in Chicago and take this down: "The Ringo Kid was killed on Main Street in Lordsburg tonight. And among the additional dead were..." Leave that blank for a spell.
clean: Ed (editor): McCoy! Billy, kill that story about the Republican Convention in Chicago and take this down: "The Ringo Kid was killed on Main Street in Lordsburg tonight. And among the additional dead were..." Leave that blank for a spell.

heading: Dialogue
raw: McCoy, typesetter: I didn't hear any shootin', Ed.
clean: McCoy, typesetter: I didn't hear any shootin', Ed.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ed (editor): You will, Billy, you will.
clean: Ed (editor): You will, Billy, you will.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dallas: Haven't I any right to live? What have I done?
clean: Haven't I any right to live? What have I done?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dr. Josiah Boone: We're the victims of a foul disease called social prejudice, my child. These dear ladies of the Law and Order League are scouring out the dregs of the town. Come on. Be a proud, glorified dreg like me.
clean: We're the victims of a foul disease called social prejudice, my child. These dear ladies of the Law and Order League are scouring out the dregs of the town. Come on. Be a proud, glorified dreg like me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marshal Curly Wilcox: You, Doc?
clean: You, Doc?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dr. Josiah Boone: I'm not only a philosopher, sir, I'm a fatalist. Somewhere, sometime, there may be the right bullet or the wrong bottle waiting for Josiah Boone. Why worry when or where?
clean: I'm not only a philosopher, sir, I'm a fatalist. Somewhere, sometime, there may be the right bullet or the wrong bottle waiting for Josiah Boone. Why worry when or where?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marshal Curly Wilcox: Yes or no?
clean: Yes or no?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dr. Josiah Boone: Having that philosophy, sir, I've always courted danger. During the late war - when I had the honor to serve the Union under our great president, Abraham Lincoln... and General Phil Sheridan - well, sir, I fought mid shot and shell and cannon roar...
clean: Having that philosophy, sir, I've always courted danger. During the late war - when I had the honor to serve the Union under our great president, Abraham Lincoln... and General Phil Sheridan - well, sir, I fought mid shot and shell and cannon roar...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marshal Curly Wilcox: Do you wanna go back or not?
clean: Do you wanna go back or not?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dr. Josiah Boone: No! I want another drink.
clean: No! I want another drink.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marshal Curly Wilcox: How 'bout you, Mr. Hancock?
clean: How 'bout you, Mr. Hancock?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Samuel Peacock: Peacock. I'd like to go on, brother. I want to reach the bosom of my dear family in Kansas City, Kansas as quickly as possible; but, I may never reach that bosom if we go on... so, under the circumstances - you understand, brother - I think it best we go back with the bosoms... I mean the soldiers.
clean: Peacock. I'd like to go on, brother. I want to reach the bosom of my dear family in Kansas City, Kansas as quickly as possible; but, I may never reach that bosom if we go on... so, under the circumstances - you understand, brother - I think it best we go back with the bosoms... I mean the soldiers.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dallas: But you don't know me - you don't know who I am.
clean: But you don't know me - you don't know who I am.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ringo Kid: I know all I wanna know. Will you go?
clean: I know all I wanna know. Will you go?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dallas: Oh, don't talk like that!
clean: Oh, don't talk like that!
